gap.missing: A public angled end-cut contract for dimensional lumber, including paired 30-degree end cuts and explicit long-point-to-long-point length semantics.
gap.evidence: The live detailgen/authoring-manifest/v2 lumber constructor publishes nominal, length, name, treated, ease_radius, holes, and full_length only; it does not publish miter_ends or an equivalent cut feature.
gap.why_required: A true equilateral 48-inch outside-edge triangle cannot be modeled as three rectangular lumber solids without either angled end cuts or a raw-geometry escape hatch.
gap.reusable_scope: Add generic semantic angled end cuts to dimensional lumber; do not add triangle- or frame-specific compiler behavior.
